purpose: Explicit admitted bridges from existing system-layer concepts into stable QIT public_ids. This registry is the conservative bridge surface used before broader cross-layer reconciliation is allowed.

bridge_policy.admission_rule: Only add bridges when the source concept is an explicit repo-held concept node and the QIT target is a stable owner public_id. Do not admit fuzzy label matches.
non_goals: Do not infer new owner truth inside the QIT graph.
Do not auto-derive bridges from text similarity.
Do not treat this registry as proof that runtime/history promotion is complete.

bridges.rationale: This refined concept is the repo-held first-principle Axis-0 anchor and is admitted as the bridge into the QIT owner axis.
This refined concept is the current system-graph anchor for engine-family inequality and is admitted as a bridge to the Type-1 engine node.
This refined concept is the current system-graph anchor for engine-family inequality and is admitted as a bridge to the Type-2 engine node.
This kernel concept explicitly anchors the Te/Ti/Fe/Fi operator family and is admitted as a bridge to the Ti owner node.
This kernel concept explicitly anchors the Te/Ti/Fe/Fi operator family and is admitted as a bridge to the Fe owner node.
This kernel concept explicitly anchors the Te/Ti/Fe/Fi operator family and is admitted as a bridge to the Te owner node.
This kernel concept explicitly anchors the Te/Ti/Fe/Fi operator family and is admitted as a bridge to the Fi owner node.
